prompt:
Bike-share ops here. The dock-ledger project stopped building somewhere in yesterday's cleanup commits (a teammate was 'modernizing properties' and also pruning old debug helpers, so the diff is noisy). `dotnet test` now gives me this pile:

  DockLedger.cs(25,29): error CS1525: Invalid expression term '{'
  DockLedger.cs(25,29): error CS1002: ; expected
  DockLedger.cs(25,29): error CS1519: Invalid token '{' in a member declaration
  DockLedger.cs(25,48): error CS1519: Invalid token '-' in a member declaration
  DockLedger.cs(25,63): error CS1519: Invalid token ';' in a member declaration
  DockLedger.cs(25,65): error CS1519: Invalid token '}' in a member declaration
  DockLedger.cs(59,1): error CS1027: #endif directive expected

That last one baffles me most — line 59 is the final brace of the file, and I count exactly as many #if lines as I ever had.

The ledger's behavior is not up for discussion: docking, releasing, the movement log, availability, utilization — all pinned by DockLedgerTests.cs and none of it was supposed to change in a cleanup. Make `dotnet test` build and pass with the smallest edits that do it. Keep the debug helper the rebalancing crew uses; don't rewrite methods that already work, and leave the tests, csproj, and .gitignore alone.
